rationale: The cited paper directly studies heterogeneous managerial strategic thinking via a cognitive hierarchy model and links higher strategic ability to better outcomes (e.g., survival), which matches the citing paper’s stated gap and contrast against prior evidence that more strategic players perform better. The citing note explicitly frames its contribution as testing when this usual positive strategic-capability effect can reverse, making this citation a plausible substantive comparison/theory benchmark rather than an incidental mention. With this strong construct overlap (strategic capability, bounded rationality/CH, performance), it should be retained for full relation judgment.
